completeness: Missing from features: AC-1.3 (UI books page), AC-3.6 (UI checkout flow), AC-5.6 (UI book detail page), AC-6.4 (UI member profile pay button) — 25/29 = 86% coverage; negatives covered for every user story (AC-1.4, AC-2.2, AC-2.4, AC-3.2-3.5, AC-4.3, AC-5.2, AC-5.3, AC-6.3); AC-4.2 uses Scenario Outline with boundary examples | days_late | 0 | 1 | 10 | 20 | 21 |
alignment: Every scenario has exactly one @AC-x.y tag (e.g., '@AC-3.2 Scenario: Reject loan when member has 5 active loans', '@AC-6.3 Scenario: Pay an already-paid fine', '@AC-4.4 Scenario: Return triggers reservation notification') — scenario names are human-readable restatements of ACs; Background blocks remove setup duplication in 5 of 6 features; 4 of 29 ACs lack a scenario, so bidirectional traceability is not complete
declarative: Good: 'When the librarian lends "Dune" to "Alice"' / 'Then the loan has a due date 14 days from now' / 'Then the member "Alice" has a fine of <expected_fine_cents> cents'

Bad (HTTP/JSON-leaking): 'Then the response status is 201' / 'Then the response status is 409' / 'And the response contains error "ISBN already exists"' / 'Then the profile shows unpaid_fines_cents of 350' / 'And the book includes reservation_queue_depth of 1'
atomic: Single-action steps throughout: 'Given the member "Alice" is suspended' / 'When the librarian returns the loan for "Dune"' / 'And the reservation status is "waiting"' — no compound 'and ... and ...' chains inside a single Given/When/Then; Background uses separate And-chained steps rather than packing multiple actions into one
